sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Honeywell says Quantinuum unit confidentially filed IPO (HON:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Honeywell announced that its quantum computing unit, Quantinuum, confidentially filed for an initial public offering on February 17. The number of shares and price range for the offering have not yet been disclosed.

feed.title: Microsoft Tests Mythos to Identify and Mitigate Vulnerabilities
feed.summary: Microsoft is collaborating with Anthropic and other partners through Project Glasswing to test Claude Mythos Preview, an AI model designed to identify and mitigate cybersecurity vulnerabilities. This initiative aims to integrate advanced AI models into Microsoft's Security Development Lifecycle to enhance threat detection and provide customers with guidance to combat AI-driven security risks. Microsoft emphasizes the importance of industry collaboration in building a safer digital environment.

feed.title: United Rentals sets May 27 payout for $1.97-a-share dividend
feed.summary: United Rentals, Inc. (NYSE: URI) announced that its Board of Directors has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.97 per share. This dividend is scheduled to be paid on May 27, 2026, to stockholders of record as of May 13, 2026. The company is the world's largest equipment rental company, operating extensively across North America, Europe, Australia, and New Zealand.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (MOH) Molina Healthcare, Inc. Posts Q1 Adjusted EPS $2.35 per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $1.90
feed.summary: Molina Healthcare, Inc. (MOH) announced its Q1 adjusted EPS reached $2.35 per share, significantly surpassing FactSet's estimate of $1.90. This financial update indicates a stronger than anticipated earnings performance for the managed healthcare provider.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (GL) Globe Life Inc. Reports Q1 Revenue $1.56B, vs. FactSet Est of $1.56B
feed.summary: Globe Life Inc. (GL) reported its first-quarter revenue at $1.56 billion, matching FactSet's estimated figure. The article also provides recent news about the company, including analyst rating adjustments and insider stock sales, alongside an overview of its business segments and financial ratings.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (CCI) Crown Castle Inc. Posts Q1 AFFO $1.02 per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $0.98
feed.summary: Crown Castle Inc. (CCI) reported Q1 Adjusted Funds From Operations (AFFO) of $1.02 per share, surpassing FactSet's estimate of $0.98. The company also provided its full-year 2026 AFFO guidance, expecting a range of $4.38 to $4.49 per share, compared to FactSet's estimated $4.47 per share. The article also includes basic company information and recent analyst ratings.

feed.title: IBM falls despite posting better-than-expected Q1 results
feed.summary: IBM's stock fell in after-hours trading despite exceeding Q1 revenue and EPS expectations. The drop is attributed to the company's Q1 earnings report omitting an internal metric previously used to track the progress of its AI business, which stood at $12.5 billion at the end of 2025. This, combined with previous concerns over Anthropic's Claude Code impacting IBM's mainframe business, contributed to investor apprehension.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (URI) United Rentals, Inc. Reports Q1 Revenue $3.98B, vs. FactSet Est of $3.87B
feed.summary: United Rentals, Inc. (URI) announced its Q1 revenue reached $3.98 billion, surpassing the FactSet estimate of $3.87 billion. The company also reported Q1 adjusted EPS of $9.71 per share, exceeding the FactSet estimate of $8.95. United Rentals expects its 2026 revenue to range between $16.9 billion and $17.4 billion, compared to FactSet's estimate of $17.07 billion.

feed.title: CF Industries Holdings, Inc. Confirms Dates for First Quarter 2026 Results and Conference Call, Announces Participation in Upcoming Investor Conference
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings, Inc. has announced the date for its first-quarter 2026 financial results release and a subsequent conference call. The company will also participate in an upcoming investor conference where its executive vice president and chief financial officer will present. These events provide opportunities for investors to gain insights into the company's financial performance and strategic outlook.

feed.title: Rollins revenue tops estimates in Q1 as Orkin parent sees seasonal demand (ROL:NYSE)
feed.summary: Rollins (ROL), the parent company of Orkin and other pest-control brands, reported first-quarter revenue of $906.4 million, exceeding Wall Street estimates, while adjusted earnings were in line with expectations. The revenue increase of 10.2% was driven by seasonal demand. This performance highlights strong operational results for the company.

feed.title: Raymond James Earnings Beat Forecasts. The Stock Is Falling.
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial reported strong fiscal second-quarter results, with adjusted net income and revenue surpassing Wall Street expectations. The wealth management company achieved adjusted earnings per share of $2.83, exceeding analyst forecasts of $2.76, primarily driven by a rise in asset-management fees and robust net new asset growth. Despite these positive financial results, the stock is currently falling.

feed.title: Lam Research stock rises nearly 3% as AI demand drives strong Q3 results, guidance
feed.summary: Lam Research Corporation reported robust third-quarter results, surpassing analyst expectations with adjusted earnings per share of $1.47 and revenue of $5.84 billion, a 24% increase year-over-year. The semiconductor equipment maker attributed its strong performance and optimistic Q4 guidance to surging AI-driven demand within the semiconductor industry. Following the announcement, the company's stock rose nearly 3%, reflecting investor confidence in its strategic investments and market outperformance.

feed.title: Kaiser Aluminum lifts 2026 forecast after $1.1B quarter
feed.summary: Kaiser Aluminum Corporation reported record first-quarter 2026 financial results, with net sales reaching $1.1 billion and a diluted EPS of $3.71. The company's strong performance, driven by increased shipments, improved pricing, and favorable product mix, led management to raise its full-year 2026 outlook for Conversion Revenue and Adjusted EBITDA by 10-15% and 20-30% year-over-year, respectively. This positive forecast reflects structural improvements and strategic initiatives taking hold across the business.

feed.title: IBM reports solid Q1, but investors focus on consulting soft spot
feed.summary: IBM exceeded Wall Street's revenue and earnings expectations for Q1 2026, driven by strong performance in software and infrastructure. However, investor focus on slightly below-expectations consulting revenue and broader concerns about enterprise technology spending led to an over 8% drop in shares during after-hours trading. Despite this, IBM reaffirmed its full-year guidance for over 5% revenue growth in constant currency.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (URI) United Rentals, Inc. Posts Q1 Adjusted EPS $9.71 per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $8.95
feed.summary: United Rentals, Inc. (URI) announced a Q1 adjusted EPS of $9.71 per share, surpassing the FactSet estimate of $8.95. This news comes alongside other updates including revised 2026 guidance and Q1 revenue of $3.98 billion, exceeding the FactSet estimate of $3.87 billion.

feed.title: Stifel lowers Moody’s stock price target on geopolitical concerns
feed.summary: Stifel has reduced its price target for Moody's Corp (NYSE:MCO) to $523 from $540, maintaining a Buy rating. This adjustment reflects increased caution due to the geopolitical environment and the Iran war, which could negatively impact the company's 2026 guidance, particularly in the MIS unit. Despite the lowered target, Stifel believes Moody's is well-positioned to leverage AI opportunities for both revenue and cost benefits, while BofA Securities recently raised its target for Moody's following strong Q1 2026 financial results.

feed.title: Iron Mountain Projects Significant Growth Ahead of Q1 2026 Earnings
feed.summary: Iron Mountain (IRM) is projected to report significant growth for Q1 2026, with analysts anticipating a 195.4% increase in Funds From Operations (FFO) to $1.27 per share. The company has consistently surpassed FFO estimates and its stock has outperformed the S&P 500 over the past year. Market sentiment is optimistic, with most analysts rating IRM as a "Strong Buy."

feed.title: Consolidated Edison Prepares to Release Fiscal Q1 2026 Earnings Results
feed.summary: Consolidated Edison (ED) is set to release its Q1 2026 earnings after market close on Thursday, May 7, with analysts projecting a diluted profit of $2.36 per share, a 4.9% increase year-over-year. Despite expected growth in EPS for 2026 and 2027, the company's stock has underperformed the S&P 500 and the utilities sector ETF over the last 52 weeks, declining by 2.2%. Market sentiment remains "Hold" among analysts, with an average price target indicating a slight potential upside.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (RJF) Raymond James Financial, Inc. Posts Q2 Adjusted EPS $2.83 per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $2.76
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial, Inc. (RJF) announced Q2 adjusted earnings per share of $2.83, surpassing FactSet's estimate of $2.76. The company also reported Q2 revenue of $3.86 billion, which exceeded FactSet's estimate of $3.85 billion. This positive earnings flash indicates a solid financial performance by Raymond James Financial for the second quarter.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Logs Higher First-Quarter Profit, Revenue
feed.summary: Texas Instruments reported increased profit and revenue in its fiscal first quarter, driven by growth in its industrial and data-center businesses. The semiconductor company also provided guidance for the current quarter that exceeded Wall Street analysts' expectations, posting a profit of $1.55 billion, or $1.68 a share. Revenue rose 19% year-over-year.

feed.title: Lam Research Stock Jumps On Q3 Record Revenue, Earnings
feed.summary: Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) reported record revenue and earnings for its third quarter, surpassing analyst estimates. The company's Q3 EPS was $1.47 on revenue of $5.84 billion, driven by AI-related demand. Following the announcement, Lam Research stock saw a 3.67% increase in extended trading, anticipating strong future performance with Q4 guidance also exceeding analyst expectations.

feed.title: Texas Instruments (TXN) Stock Rises on Q1 2026 Earnings
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TXN) reported strong Q1 2026 earnings, with revenue up 18.6% year-over-year to $4.8 billion and diluted EPS up 31.3% to $1.68, both exceeding analyst estimates. Following these positive results, the company's shares rose by 8.12%. The article also details recent insider trading activity, institutional holdings changes, congressional stock trading, and current analyst ratings and price targets for TXN.

feed.title: BofA raises DTE Energy stock price target to $162 on growth outlook
feed.summary: BofA Securities has increased its price target for DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) to $162 from $155, while maintaining a Buy rating. This adjustment reflects an optimistic outlook on the utility company's growth, despite an expected slight dip in first-quarter 2026 operating earnings per share. The company is poised to reaffirm its 2026 full-year EPS guidance and a significant capital plan, excluding a Google datacenter, and boasts a consistent dividend yield.

feed.title: SOUTHWEST AIRLINES REPORTS FIRST QUARTER RESULTS, BUSINESS TRANSFORMATION INITIATIVES DELIVER MEANINGFUL MARGIN EXPANSION
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines announced strong first-quarter 2026 results, with a net income of $227 million and significant margin expansion. The company attributes this success to its business transformation initiatives, which drove record revenues and double-digit unit revenue growth, despite higher fuel costs. Southwest Airlines also provided guidance for Q2 2026, expecting adjusted EPS between $0.35 and $0.65, while maintaining a disciplined capacity plan.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Teledyne Technologies (TDY) After Its Strong Share Price Run?
feed.summary: Teledyne Technologies (TDY) has seen significant share price appreciation, raising questions about its current valuation. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock may be overvalued by 12.6%, while its P/E ratio of 33.27x also appears richer than the proprietary Fair Ratio. The article encourages investors to use "Narratives" on Simply Wall St's platform to develop their own valuation based on future projections and compare them to analyst targets.

feed.title: Is Eastman Chemical (EMN) Now Attractively Priced After Recent Share Price Strength
feed.summary: Eastman Chemical (EMN), currently trading around US$72.46, is analyzed using various valuation methods. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is significantly undervalued by 46.0%, with an intrinsic value of approximately $134.29 per share. The company's P/E ratio of 17.5x is also below the Chemicals industry average and Simply Wall St's proprietary Fair Ratio of 23.0x, further indicating it might be attractively priced, especially considering different investor narratives.

feed.title: A Look At Booking Holdings (BKNG) Valuation As Earnings Expectations And Stock Split Raise Investor Interest
feed.summary: Booking Holdings (BKNG) is under scrutiny as it approaches its earnings report, anticipated to reveal increased year-over-year profit and revenue, following a 25-for-1 stock split. Despite trading at a discount to some analyst targets, with a reported fair value significantly higher than its current share price, investors are debating whether its strong growth is already priced in. The article highlights Booking's transition from a "reopening trade" to a "platform durability story," supported by evolving travel behavior, integrated services, and scale advantages.

feed.title: The Bull Case For Amgen (AMGN) Could Change Following Strong Q4 Beat And Upbeat 2026 Guidance
feed.summary: Amgen reported strong Q4 2025 results, beating revenue and adjusted EPS expectations, and provided upbeat guidance for 2026. The article discusses how this performance and guidance might impact the investment narrative for Amgen, especially with upcoming Q1 2026 results where analysts predict an earnings dip. It also touches on Amgen's broad portfolio, pipeline, and efforts to counteract pricing pressure and patent loss.

feed.title: Northern Trust shareholders elect board, approve compensation and audit firm
feed.summary: Northern Trust Corporation shareholders convened their 2026 annual meeting, where they elected 13 directors to the board, approved 2025 executive officer compensation, and ratified the 2026 Employee Stock Purchase Plan. Shareholders also reappointed KPMG LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for the current fiscal year. These decisions come amidst recent expansions in the company's financial services and positive adjustments to its stock outlook by several analyst firms.

feed.title: After a $4B quarter, United Rentals raises its 2026 forecast
feed.summary: United Rentals (NYSE: URI) announced record first-quarter 2026 financial results, including nearly $4 billion in total revenue and diluted EPS of $8.43, prompting the company to raise its full-year 2026 guidance. The company highlighted strong growth across its general rentals and specialty businesses, fueled by momentum heading into the busy season and opportunities in large projects. United Rentals returned $500 million to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends and reaffirmed its commitment to delivering industry-leading growth and shareholder value.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Tops Q1 Estimates, Strong Guidance Sparks Stock Surge
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (NASDAQ: TXN) reported Q1 revenue of $4.83 billion and earnings of $1.68 per share, both exceeding analyst estimates. The company provided strong Q2 guidance, forecasting revenue between $5 billion and $5.40 billion and earnings from $1.77 to $2.05 per share. Following the positive report, Texas Instruments stock surged over 7% in after-hours trading.

feed.title: CSX: Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: CSX Corp. reported first-quarter earnings of $807 million, or 43 cents per share, surpassing Wall Street's expectations of 39 cents per share. However, the freight railroad's revenue of $3.48 billion missed analysts' forecasts of $3.51 billion. This financial report was generated by Automated Insights using data from Zacks Investment Research.

feed.title: TMX deal to buy Cboe exchanges in Canada and Australia raises questions about competition
feed.summary: TMX Group Ltd. is acquiring the Canadian and Australian assets of Cboe Global Markets Inc. for US$300-million, a move that raises concerns about competition in the Canadian capital markets given TMX's already dominant position. While TMX CEO John McKenzie believes the deal will increase efficiency and lead to savings for the industry, critics argue it risks creating a monopoly and hindering innovation. The transaction will be reviewed by the federal Competition Bureau.

feed.title: Bank of Nova Scotia reduces KeyCorp (NYSE: KEY) stake via issuer deal
feed.summary: Bank of Nova Scotia, a director and significant owner of KeyCorp (NYSE: KEY), has reduced its stake by disposing of 49,921 common shares at $21.95 per share. This transaction was a "disposition to the issuer" under an existing Investment Agreement, meaning the shares were returned to KeyCorp rather than sold on the open market. Following this deal on April 21, 2026, Bank of Nova Scotia now holds 159,826,131 KeyCorp common shares directly.

feed.title: Fifth Third Stakes Claim in Texas with First Branch Opening
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bancorp has opened its first Texas financial center in Frisco, marking the start of a major expansion with more than $700 million in planned investment. The bank aims to grow its retail presence to over 250 locations by 2029, securing a top-five footprint in Dallas, Austin, and Houston, building on an existing presence from Comerica. This expansion is part of a national strategy to increase its retail locations across 17 of the 20 fastest-growing large markets in the U.S.

feed.title: ServiceNow Posts Lukewarm Sales on Middle East Deal Delays
feed.summary: ServiceNow Inc. reported lukewarm financial results, with subscription revenue increasing 22% to $3.67 billion, which was in line with analyst estimates. However, the company noted that its growth rate would have been nearly 1 percentage point higher if not for delayed sales deals in the Middle East due to ongoing conflict, causing its shares to drop approximately 13% in extended trading.

feed.title: Wind turbine bearing pads can be replaced uptower, without cranes
feed.summary: Waukesha Bearings, a part of Dover, has launched NordAlign, an innovative bearing system for wind turbine main shafts. This new technology allows for maintenance and replacement of bearing pads uptower, eliminating the need for expensive jack-up vessels and cranes, thereby reducing operational costs and downtime. The NordAlign bearing, featuring TruTech engineered polymer pads and a modular design, aims to improve reliability and simplify installation for both new and retrofit wind turbine nacelles.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks Inc Stock (PANW) Closed Up by 3.66% on Apr 22: Facts Behind the Movement
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks (PANW) stock rose by 3.66% on April 22, driven by a "strong-buy" upgrade from Berenberg, strategic advancements in AI security, and strong industry demand for cybersecurity solutions. The company's involvement in Anthropic's Project Glasswing and the acquisition of Koi have bolstered investor confidence, despite some intraday volatility stemming from valuation concerns and integration risks related to recent acquisitions. Technical and fundamental analyses show mixed signals, but positive catalysts pushed the stock higher.

feed.title: A $93M impairment hit Molina's quarter; 2026 targets stayed intact
feed.summary: Molina Healthcare reported Q1 2026 adjusted EPS of $2.35, reaffirming its full-year 2026 premium revenue and adjusted earnings guidance despite a 95% decrease in GAAP net income due to a $93 million impairment charge. The impairment relates to the company's planned exit from the Medicare Advantage-Part D product for 2027. The company's Medical Care Ratio increased to 91.1%, and premium revenue decreased by 4% year-over-year.

feed.title: KKR Real Estate Finance puts Q1 results online before Apr. 23 call
feed.summary: KKR Real Estate Finance Trust Inc. (KREF) has released its first-quarter 2026 results, which are now available on the Investor Relations section of its website. The company will host a conference call on Thursday, April 23, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. ET to discuss these financial results. KREF, externally managed by an affiliate of KKR & Co. Inc., specializes in originating and acquiring senior loans secured by commercial real estate properties.

feed.title: KKR Real Estate Finance Trust Inc. Reports First Quarter 2026 Results
feed.summary: KKR Real Estate Finance Trust Inc. (KREF) has released its first-quarter 2026 financial results, which are now available on its investor relations website. The company will host a conference call on April 23, 2026, to discuss these results, accessibility via dial-in or live webcast. Additionally, KREF declared a quarterly preferred stock dividend of $0.40625 per share.

feed.title: Rollins: Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Rollins Inc. (ROL) reported a first-quarter profit of $107.8 million, or 22 cents per share, which matched Wall Street expectations with adjusted earnings of 24 cents per share. The company, known for its pest control services, also exceeded revenue forecasts, posting $906.4 million against an estimated $895.1 million. This financial performance indicates a strong start to the year for the Atlanta-based operator of Orkin.

feed.title: Raymond James adds $23B in client assets as revenue hits record
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial (NYSE: RJF) reported record fiscal second-quarter net revenues of $3.86 billion, a 13% increase year-over-year, and net income of $542 million ($2.72 diluted EPS). The Private Client Group added $23.0 billion in net new assets, bringing total client assets under administration to $1.76 trillion and record fee-based assets to $1.04 trillion. The company also announced share repurchases of $400 million during the quarter.

feed.title: Top Takeaways From Seatrade Cruise Global 2026
feed.summary: The 40th Seatrade Cruise Global conference in Miami highlighted major developments across the cruise industry, including significant fleet expansions and renovations. AmaWaterways announced plans to grow its riverboat fleet to over 50 by 2032, while Azamara Cruises detailed a fleet-wide renovation initiative, Azamara Forward, starting with Azamara Quest. Holland America Line unveiled a multiyear, $500 million investment, "Holland America Evolution," to enhance six ships, and Princess Cruises announced an order for three new Voyager Class vessels. MSC Cruises and Explora Journeys revealed plans for a new luxury private island, Sandy Cay, and Riverside Luxury Cruises confirmed the full integration of Riverside Mahler into its fleet.

feed.title: Arista Networks Inc Stock (ANET) Closed Up by 3.04% on Apr 22: Key Drivers Unveiled
feed.summary: Arista Networks Inc (ANET) saw its stock rise by 3.04% on April 22, driven by strong analyst sentiment and expectations of robust financial performance, particularly in the AI and cloud networking sectors. The company doubled its 2026 AI networking revenue target and is anticipated to beat Q1 2026 earnings estimates. Despite a premium valuation and some insider selling, the overall outlook for ANET remains positive due to AI-driven growth and institutional investor interest.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (LVS) Las Vegas Sands Corp. Reports Q1 Revenue $3.585B, vs. FactSet Est of $3.36B
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands Corp. announced Q1 revenue of $3.585 billion, surpassing FactSet's estimate of $3.36 billion. The company also reported Q1 adjusted EPS of $0.91 per share, exceeding the FactSet estimate of $0.76. Additionally, Las Vegas Sands maintained its quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Walmart to remodel stores across Southern California
feed.summary: Walmart plans to remodel 56 stores across California this year, focusing on improving store layouts, technology, and expanding services to enhance the shopping experience. Specific stores in Los Angeles, Orange, and Riverside counties are slated for upgrades, which include better in-store and online shopping features, updated pharmacies, and expanded product selections. These remodels are part of a larger investment strategy in California, aiming to modernize stores and better serve communities.

feed.title: Las Vegas Sands Reports Q1 2026: Revenue $3.59B, Net Income $641M, Adj. Property EBITDA $1.42B
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands (LVS) reported strong first-quarter 2026 results, with net revenue reaching $3.59 billion and net income at $641 million, significantly up from the prior year. The company's consolidated adjusted property EBITDA grew to $1.42 billion, and diluted EPS was $0.85, supported by growth in both Singapore and Macao operations. LVS also highlighted a continued return of capital to shareholders, including $740 million in common stock repurchases during the quarter.

feed.title: Athleisure company Lululemon appoints Heidi O'Neill as new top boss
feed.summary: Lululemon has reportedly appointed Heidi O'Neill, a former Nike executive, as its new CEO. The appointment, reported by the Wall Street Journal, tasks O'Neill with addressing declining sales, refreshing designs, and navigating internal company issues.

feed.title: D.R. Horton (NYSE: DHI) director exercises RSUs into common shares
feed.summary: D.R. Horton director Benjamin Carson Sr exercised 683 Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) into common shares on April 20, 2026, as part of routine equity compensation. These transactions increase his direct holdings to 9,752 shares, with an additional 278 RSUs remaining. The conversion reflects previously granted vesting schedules and is not indicative of open-market trading.
